paper_title: Recovering Temporal Regulatory Rewiring: An Interpretable Graph-Based Virtual Perturbation Framework Applied to Ischemic Stroke
authors: Ming Luo, Yimin Mei, Wangyang Ye, Wenwu Huang
institution: The Fifth Affiliated Hospital of Wenzhou Medical University (Lishui Central Hospital), Lishui, Zhejiang, China
field: Bioinformatics / Computational Biology — gene-regulatory networks, single-cell transcriptomics, graph neural networks; applied to Neuroscience (ischemic stroke)
methodology: Method/framework paper (computational, no wet-lab). Validated across two mouse cohorts, human GRN convergence, and three independent perturbation modalities with permutation testing.
key_contribution: TSC-GNN recovers edge-level GRN rewiring and master-regulator dynamics from multi-timepoint single-cell transcriptomes. The contribution is interpretability, not accuracy: under a fixed causal graph with a linear readout, graph topology does not beat linear baselines (0/90 configurations).
keywords: gene-regulatory network; temporal rewiring; virtual perturbation; graph neural network; single-cell transcriptomics; ischemic stroke; interpretability; edge-level rewiring; drug repurposing; cross-species regulatory conservation; master-regulator inference
indexing_pref: Dual-indexed (Scopus + Web of Science); Open Access / Subscription unrestricted
journals_found: 31 journals curated and tiered below, drawn from 179 dual-indexed candidates matching the bioinformatics / computational-biology scope

target.name: IEEE/ACM Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics

ambitious_notes.Nature Methods: The flagship venue for new life-science methods; TSC-GNN's edge-level rewiring interpretability and open code with SHA-256 manifests fit its primary-methods mandate, though its GNN-for-transcriptomics scope is more computational than the typical wet-lab method.
ambitious_notes.Nature Biotechnology: Publishes technology/methodology advances with translational reach; the drug-repurposing (HDACi / statins) angle gives a translational hook, but the all-computational, no-validated-assay design is a stretch for this journal's remit.
ambitious_notes.Nature Communications: Broad OA megajournal that regularly hosts computational methods with cross-species and clinical translation; the multi-cohort plus human-blood activation story fits its 'important advance' bar.
ambitious_notes.Genome Biology: A top OA journal for genomics methods and software; the GRN-rewiring framework and reproducible pipeline align tightly with its methods-and-tools scope.
ambitious_notes.Cell Genomics: Cell Press OA genomics journal; the temporal single-cell GRN rewiring in stroke is squarely within its genome-technology-and-biology mandate.
ambitious_notes.Cell Systems: Systems-level life-science methods venue; the graph-based interpretable perturbation framework matches its systems-understanding remit, though it leans more toward wet-lab systems work.
ambitious_notes.Nucleic Acids Research: Home of databases / web servers and computational genomics resources; a strong fit if TSC-GNN is packaged as a usable web server or resource, less so as a pure methods paper.
ambitious_notes.GigaScience: OA big-data methods journal; the fully reproducible conda + SHA-256 pipeline and public-data focus map well to its open-data mission (live IF not retrieved).

target_notes.Bioinformatics: The canonical home for bioinformatics methods; temporal GRN rewiring plus GNN readout is exactly its sweet spot, and the OA option aids visibility.
target_notes.PLOS Computational Biology: OA comp-bio methods journal with a strong single-cell / GNN readership; ideal if you want maximum openness and a methods-friendly review track.
target_notes.Briefings in Bioinformatics: High-IF review-style venue; best if the paper is framed as a methodological review / position piece with the TSC-GNN case study, rather than a pure original-method report.
target_notes.BMC Bioinformatics: Reliable OA methods journal; a pragmatic, high-acceptance home for the algorithmic pipeline and benchmarks.
target_notes.Cell Reports Methods: Cell Press OA methods journal; purpose-built for reproducible computational protocols like the TSC-GNN pipeline and its rewiring benchmark.
target_notes.npj Systems Biology and Applications: Nature-branded OA systems-biology venue; the GRN-rewiring-as-recovery-program framing matches its scope (IF ~3.6).
target_notes.NAR Genomics and Bioinformatics: OUP OA genomics + bioinformatics journal; good fit for the single-cell GRN method, though lower IF (2.8) and JCR Q2.
target_notes.Bioinformatics Advances: OUP OA companion to Bioinformatics for solid-but-narrower methods; a safe, on-scope landing spot (IF 2.6).
target_notes.Genomics, Proteomics and Bioinformatics: BGI / OUP OA journal with rising IF (7.2, Q1); strong fit for the multi-omics GRN rewiring story and China-based authorship.
target_notes.Molecular Systems Biology: EMBO OA systems-biology flagship; excellent fit for network-rewiring biology if the biological (not just computational) narrative is strengthened (live IF not retrieved).
target_notes.IEEE/ACM Transactions on Computational B: Society venue for algorithmic / statistical comp-bio; fits the GNN method core but is lower-prestige than the biology-native options (live IF not retrieved).
target_notes.Journal of Computational Biology: Long-standing comp-bio methods journal; a dependable, lower-tier home for the algorithmic contribution (live IF not retrieved).

safe_notes.Frontiers in Bioinformatics: OA frontier journal, fast and broad; a low-risk home for the method with decent visibility (IF 3.6).
safe_notes.BMC Genomics: Broad OA genomics journal; suitable if the paper is angled toward the stroke transcriptomics findings rather than the method.
safe_notes.BioData Mining: BMC OA journal for biomedical ML / data-mining; fits the ML + single-cell angle (IF 6.1, higher than typical Safe).
safe_notes.Scientific Reports: Nature Portfolio mega-journal, very high acceptance; a dependable safety net for a complete, reproducible method (IF 3.8).
safe_notes.Computers in Biology and Medicine: Elsevier journal for biomedical ML; good topical fit but was placed On Hold by WoS in 2024 — verify its current indexing status before submitting (IF ~7.0).
safe_notes.Human Genomics: OA genomics journal; relevant if the human-stroke-blood activation result is foregrounded (IF 4.3).
safe_notes.Frontiers in Computational Neuroscience: OA neuroscience-methods journal; fits the stroke / rewiring application angle, lower IF (2.3).
safe_notes.Frontiers in Neuroinformatics: OA neuroinformatics venue; appropriate if the software / tooling aspect is emphasized (IF 2.5).
safe_notes.Algorithms for Molecular Biology: BMC OA algorithms journal; a niche but on-scope home for the graph method (IF 1.7).
safe_notes.Computational Biology and Chemistry: Elsevier journal for computational life sciences; acceptable fit, moderate tier (IF 3.4).
safe_notes.Journal of Integrative Bioinformatics: OA integrative-bioinformatics journal; a modest, near-guaranteed-acceptance fallback (IF ~2.0).

quality.novelty_txt: TSC-GNN is a novel synthesis of state-conditioned GRNs, GNN message-passing, and edge-level rewiring interpretability; it extends (rather than invents) each component but introduces a distinct recovery-oriented virtual-perturbation framing.
quality.methodology_txt: Validated across two mouse cohorts, human GRN convergence, and three independent perturbation modalities with explicit permutation testing; limited only by the absence of wet-lab confirmation.
quality.breadth_txt: Advances the interpretable virtual-perturbation sub-field applied to stroke recovery; impact is concentrated rather than field-wide.
quality.evidence_txt: Longitudinal 24 h to 14 d design, cross-cohort reproducibility (rho = 0.48-0.55), and cross-species triangulation give robust computational evidence, though entirely in silico.
quality.clarity_txt: The manuscript openly reports that a fixed graph plus linear readout does not beat linear baselines (0/90), reframing the contribution as interpretability - a clear, honest benchmark.
quality.verdict: Best realistic fit is the Target tier. Ambitious journals are aspirational reaches: the central contribution is interpretability rather than accuracy gains, which top methods venues weigh heavily. Lead with Bioinformatics (Oxford), Cell Reports Methods, or PLOS Computational Biology.
